author: @ericreiter1
text: Why is the audio so terrible on your videos?  Put the patent in place to stop the patent troll? Maybe but I do not understand.

author: @mouldykev
text: ah i have finally seen the light at long last and to be honest it will be due to two things: 1  the power of the internet, in being able to pass on to the masses your ideas or invention..........2 as my mate Peter Middleton said just give it away freely to the masses, then no one can stop it !

author: @gr1f1th
text: I use to work as a Litigation Animator and one of the the last ones I worked on revealed a horrible tactic wherein you can inject new "specific criteria" into a patent that loosely pertains to the subject. The patent would then predate the patent that they want to claim infringement upon.  Rendering patenting superfluous.  You can patent and still get sued and lose. Everything is so broken. :(

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no way - that is a joke! - thanks for that mate - fascinating in a horrible way lol

author: @focojeepr
text: Robert, you have outlined the only non-coercive reason to have a patent, but there are better ways such as open source licensing, better yet elimination of all patent laws.

author: @bartoszbarejko1585
text: Are the any countries that do not care about patents?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good point - Finland has recently passed legislation restricting what can and can't be patented
